title: 10 Herbal Creams For Bruises
intro_desc: Herbal creams for bruises have become a popular alternative to traditional treatments. They work by reducing inflammation and promoting healing. One of the key herbs used in these creams is Arnica montana, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and swelling.  When applied topically, Arnica can help to speed up the healing process, allowing bruises to fade faster. Another herb, Aloe barbadensis, is also commonly used in creams for its soothing and cooling effects. Aloe helps to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it an excellent addition to any bruise treatment.  Calendula officinalis is another herb that's often used in herbal creams for bruises. It has antiseptic properties that help to prevent infection and promote healing. When applied to the skin, Calendula can help to reduce redness and swelling, making it an effective treatment for bruises. By using herbal creams containing these herbs, you can reduce the appearance of bruises and promote faster healing.  These herbal creams can be especially beneficial for people who are sensitive to chemicals or have allergies. They're also a great option for those who prefer natural treatments. By incorporating herbal creams into your daily routine, you can reduce the appearance of bruises and promote healthy skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams contains flavoglycosides, thiol esters, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are the key active constituents that help with bruising. Flavoglycosides reduce inflammation by slowing down the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause swelling. Thiol esters, on the other hand, have a direct anti-inflammatory effect by blocking the production of these prostaglandins. Sesquiterpene lactones also have anti-inflammatory properties by inhibiting the enzymes that cause pain and swelling. By reducing inflammation and pain, Arnica montana creams can help bruises heal faster and more effectively.
remedies.image_alt: Arnica montana herbal creams for bruises
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of arnica oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil, beeswax, and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly. Stir in the arnica oil and vitamin E oil until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a small bowl or container and let it cool completely. This will take about 30 minutes to an hour.
Once the mixture has cooled and solidified, you can apply it to bruises as needed. Store any leftover cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains bioactive constituents like aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and promote healing in bruises. The aloe-emodin in Aloe barbadensis creams has been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory enzymes, which can contribute to bruising and pain. The antioxidant properties of acemannan help protect the skin from further damage and promote collagen production, which can aid in the healing process. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Aloe barbadensis creams can help alleviate the appearance and discomfort of bruises.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for bruises
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1 tablespoon of aloe vera gel, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler or in the microwave for 10-15 seconds.
Combine the melted beeswax, coconut oil, and aloe vera gel in a bowl. Stir until the mixture is smooth.
Add the distilled water to the mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Use as a cream to soothe bruises.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains boiactive constituents like triterpene saponins, sesquiterpene lactones, and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and promote healing in bruises. The sesquiterpene lactones in Calendula officinalis creams also have a soothing effect on the skin, which can help calm the pain and discomfort associated with bruising. The triterpene saponins in the cream have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infection and promote a clean environment for healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Calendula officinalis creams can help bruises heal faster and more effectively.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for bruises
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of water, 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Infuse the Calendula flowers in the water for 2 hours or overnight. Strain the mixture and discard the flowers.
In a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl, melt the beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter. Stir until smooth.
Add the infused water to the melted mixture and stir well. Remove from heat.
Let the mixture cool and thicken. Pour into a container and store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams contains active constituents like allantoin, aloe-emodin, and mucilages. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help reduce swelling and pain in bruises. Allantoin in particular promotes cell growth and regeneration, which aids in the healing process of damaged tissues. The mucilages in Symphytum officinale creams also form a protective barrier on the skin's surface, shielding it from further irritation and promoting a smooth recovery. By reducing inflammation and promoting tissue regeneration, Symphytum officinale creams can help bruises heal faster and more effectively.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal creams for bruises
remedy_procedure: Gather 250g of Symphytum officinale root, 1 cup of water, and a saucepan.
Boil the water in the saucepan and add the Symphytum officinale root. Let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture and let it cool down. Then, mix it with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture until the beeswax is melted and well combined. Remove from heat and let it cool down slightly.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool down completely. Once set, your Symphytum officinale cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains flavonoids, such as quercetin and rutin, and naphthoquinones, like hyperforin and hypericin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling and promote healing in bruises. The flavonoids in Hypericum perforatum creams also have a vasoconstrictive effect, which helps to reduce blood flow to the affected area and prevent further bruising. The antioxidants in the cream can neutralize free radicals, which can damage skin cells and slow down the healing process. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Hypericum perforatum creams can help bruises to recover faster and more effectively.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for bruises
remedy_procedure: Gather 100g of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers and 1 cup of coconut oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the coconut oil over low heat and add the dried Hypericum perforatum flowers.
Let the mixture simmer for 2-3 hours, then strain it through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Let the mixture cool, then add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Mix well and pour the cream into a container, let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana creams contains a compound called astringent tannins, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in bruises. The high concentration of flavonoids, particularly quercetin, in Hamamelis virginiana creams has antioxidant properties that fight free radicals and promote healing. The mucilages in the cream also help soothe and calm the skin, reducing pain and discomfort associated with bruises. The anti-inflammatory properties of Hamamelis virginiana creams, attributed to the presence of phenolic acids and glycosides, aid in reducing redness and swelling. By applying Hamamelis virginiana creams to bruises, the combination of these bioactive constituents can help promote a faster recovery and reduce the appearance of bruising.
remedies.image_alt: Hamamelis virginiana herbal creams for bruises
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Hamamelis virginiana (witch hazel) flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Heat the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, stirring until the beeswax is melted.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Add the Hamamelis virginiana flowers, vitamin E oil, and shea butter. Stir well.
Let the mixture cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes. Stir occasionally to help it cool evenly.
Once the mixture is set, use a spatula to scoop it into a clean container. Let it cool completely before using as a cream for bruises.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains bioactive constituents like alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and promote healing of bruises. Alkylamides, specifically echinacoside and iso-echinacoside, have been shown to inhibit the release of inflammatory mediators, which can help minimize bruising. The glycosides, like chicoric acid, have antioxidant properties that help protect damaged tissues from further harm. By applying Echinacea purpurea creams to bruises, the bioactive constituents can help alleviate inflammation, promote tissue repair, and speed up the healing process.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for bruises
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Dry the Echinacea flowers in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours. Let them cool completely.
In a double boiler, melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax. Stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Add the dried Echinacea flowers and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your Echinacea purpurea cream is ready to use on bruises.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago major creams contains aucubin, baicalein, and caffeic acid as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and promote healing in bruises. Aucubin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, while baicalein and caffeic acid have strong antioxidant effects that help to protect the skin from damage. The anti-inflammatory properties of these compounds help to reduce the redness and swelling associated with bruises, while their antioxidant effects promote the healing process. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Plantago major creams can help to speed up the recovery process for bruises.
remedies.image_alt: Plantago major herbal creams for bruises
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Plantago major leaves, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Blend the Plantago major leaves in a blender or food processor until they are well chopped.
In a saucepan, melt the coconut oil and beeswax over low heat. Stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Add the chopped Plantago major leaves to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, use as a cream for bruise treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium myrtillus creams contains anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are powerful antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and promote healing. These antioxidants work by neutralizing free radicals that can damage skin cells and slow down the healing process. The anthocyanins in Vaccinium myrtillus creams also have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with bruises. Furthermore, the flavonoids and phenolic acids in these creams have been shown to improve blood flow and promote the formation of new collagen, which helps to repair damaged skin. As a result, Vaccinium myrtillus creams can help to reduce the appearance of bruises and promote faster healing.
remedies.image_alt: Vaccinium myrtillus herbal creams for bruises
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Vaccinium myrtillus berries, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1 tablespoon of beeswax.
Wash the Vaccinium myrtillus berries in a fine-mesh strainer under cold water.
In a blender or food processor, mix the Vaccinium myrtillus berries until they release their juice.
In a small saucepan, melt the coconut oil and beeswax over low heat. Remove from heat and mix in the Vaccinium myrtillus juice.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids, bilobalide, and ginkgolides as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce the swelling and discoloration of bruises. The flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba creams have been shown to improve blood flow to the affected area, promoting faster healing and reducing the time it takes for bruises to fade. The antioxidant properties also help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate bruising. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Ginkgo biloba creams can be an effective treatment for bruises.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for bruises
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments.
Add 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract and 10 drops of vitamin E oil to the melted mixture.
Add the melted beeswax to the mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a small tin or container and let it cool and harden before use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help reduce bruising. Some herbal teas, like ginger and turmeric, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and promote healing. These teas may also improve circulation, which can help bring nutrients to damaged blood vessels and speed up the healing process.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for bruises daily can be safe, but it's essential to choose teas with gentle properties. Some herbal teas, like calendula and plantain, may irritate skin if used excessively. Start with small amounts and observe your body's response. If you have sensitive skin, consider alternating tea days to avoid any potential issues.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with bruises, but it takes some time to see results. Typically, you might notice a difference in a few days to a week. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and promote healing. As the bruise starts to fade, you'll see a decrease in its size and color.
faq_time: If you're looking to help bruises heal, consider drinking herbal tea in the morning. This allows the antioxidants and other beneficial compounds to work throughout the day, helping to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Drinking it in the morning can give you a boost and support your body's natural healing processes.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for bruises, especially blood thinners like warfarin. Some teas, like ginkgo biloba and garlic tea, can increase the risk of bleeding. If you're taking blood thinners, talk to your pharmacist about potential interactions with herbal teas, like chamomile or peppermint, to avoid any complications.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas, especially for bruises. Some herbal teas might have effects on your body that could affect your baby. If you're concerned about bruises, you can try cold or warm compresses to reduce swelling. You can also talk to your pharmacist about safe alternatives to herbal teas.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's best to check the ingredients of herbal teas before drinking them. Some herbal teas, like comfrey, contain compounds that can affect a baby's liver. Other teas, like chamomile and peppermint, are often considered safe, but still, it's a good idea to check the specific ingredients and amounts used.
title_2: 10 Herbal Creams to Help Elderly with Bruises
